summary: Entry-level software engineer with hands-on internship experience building and deploying production systems using Python, SQL, Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud infrastructure. Comfortable across the full development lifecycle, from designing data pipelines and relational schemas to containerizing services and automating CI/CD workflows. Eager to contribute to collaborative engineering teams working on scalable, high-quality software.

skills.ml: YOLOv5, DistilBERT, XGBoost, LangChain, RAG, OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, HuggingFace, SHAP, Captum
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, GCP, Railway, Vercel, Supabase (PostgreSQL), CI/CD pipelines
skills.frontend: Next.js, React, REST APIs, FastAPI, Express

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Containerized a YOLO-based object detection model with Docker and deployed it on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable load without code changes.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

Built a data augmentation pipeline using Python, NumPy, and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; accuracy improved from 65% to 85.4% on held-out evaluation data.

Developed a YOLO-based object detection model in Python to detect and classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Deployed a full-stack application across Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security and Magic Link authentication), with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation, demonstrating end-to-end ownership of cloud infrastructure and deployment.

Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and a post-interview evaluation system scoring candidates across 4 dimensions with advance/hold/reject output.

Designed a multi-turn conversational AI engine with dynamic context injection: the LLM receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time.

Engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6, achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Maintained a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds a Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main, keeping the release process automated and consistent.
Maintained 35+ pytest unit tests and a 55-question domain evaluation set, supporting ongoing quality assurance and defect analysis throughout development.

Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.

Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance; integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI).

Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces using GCP and Docker.
